title: Complete Web Development Course
description: Learn the foundations of modern web development with HTML structure, CSS styling, and JavaScript interactivity in one structured path.

lectures.title: HTML Foundations — Structure & Semantics
lectures.description: Documents, headings, lists, links, images, and semantic HTML for accessible pages.

lectures.title: CSS Essentials — Layout & Styling
lectures.description: Selectors, the box model, Flexbox, responsive basics, and consistent typography.

lectures.title: JavaScript Basics — Interactivity
lectures.description: Variables, DOM manipulation, events, and small interactive components.

questions.question: Which HTML element is most appropriate for the main heading of a page?
questions.explanation: Use a single <h1> for the primary heading; <title> is metadata and appears in the tab.

questions.question: In CSS, which property controls the space inside an element, between content and border?
questions.explanation: Padding is inside the border; margin is outside.

questions.question: Which selector targets an element by its id attribute?
questions.explanation: The # prefix denotes an ID selector in CSS.

questions.question: What does CSS Flexbox primarily help you arrange?
options: Items along one dimension (row or column)
questions.explanation: Flexbox is a one-dimensional layout method for aligning and distributing space among items.

questions.question: Which HTML attribute provides alternative text for images (accessibility & SEO)?
questions.explanation: The alt attribute describes the image when it cannot be displayed or for screen readers.

title: SEO Mastery — Rank #1 on Google
description: Master keyword strategy, on-page optimization, technical SEO basics, and measuring performance to improve organic visibility.

lectures.title: Keyword Research & Search Intent
lectures.description: Finding topics, mapping intent, and prioritizing keywords your audience actually searches.

lectures.title: On-Page SEO — Content & Meta
lectures.description: Titles, headings, internal linking, and structured content that search engines understand.

lectures.title: Technical SEO Basics & Measurement
lectures.description: Crawlability, Core Web Vitals overview, Search Console, and tracking meaningful SEO KPIs.

questions.question: What does ‘search intent’ refer to?
options: Why a user runs a query and what result they expect
Your ad budget on Google Ads
The number of backlinks you have
questions.explanation: Aligning content with intent (informational, navigational, transactional, etc.) improves relevance and rankings.

questions.question: Which practice best describes a primary keyword?
options: A hidden keyword only bots can see
The main topic phrase you want a page to rank for
Any word repeated 50 times on the page
A keyword only used in image filenames
questions.explanation: The primary keyword reflects the core topic; use it naturally in title, headings, and body.

questions.question: What is a meta description primarily used for?
options: To replace your page title in rankings
To summarize the page in search snippets (can influence click-through)
To block Google from indexing the page
questions.explanation: Meta descriptions can appear under the blue link in results and encourage clicks when compelling.

questions.question: Why are internal links important for SEO?
options: They replace the need for backlinks
They help discovery, distribute authority, and clarify site structure
They increase server load for better rankings
questions.explanation: Internal linking connects related pages and helps users and crawlers navigate your site.

questions.question: Google Search Console is mainly useful for:
options: Insights on indexing, queries, and technical coverage issues
questions.explanation: Search Console shows how Google sees your property: coverage, queries, and actionable diagnostics.

title: Complete Digital Marketing Course
description: Learn Social Media Marketing, Content Creation, Facebook & Instagram Ads, Twitter strategy, and Audit Reports. Practical sessions with real examples.

lectures.title: Digital Marketing Introduction
lectures.description: Getting started with digital marketing fundamentals

lectures.title: Marketing Strategy Session
lectures.description: Building your marketing strategy from scratch

lectures.title: Advanced Marketing Techniques
lectures.description: Deep dive into advanced digital marketing methods

lectures.title: Social Media Marketing
lectures.description: Facebook, Instagram and Twitter marketing strategies

lectures.title: Facebook, Instagram & Twitter Audit Report
lectures.description: How to create and analyze social media audit reports

lectures.title: Content Creation Masterclass
lectures.description: Creating engaging content for all social media platforms

questions.question: What is Digital Marketing?
options: Marketing products through digital channels like internet and social media
questions.explanation: Digital marketing covers all marketing efforts that use the internet or electronic devices including social media, email, SEO, and paid ads.

questions.question: Which platform has the most monthly active users?
questions.explanation: Facebook has over 3 billion monthly active users, making it the largest social media platform.

questions.question: What is a Social Media Audit?
options: A review of your social media presence, performance and strategy
questions.explanation: A social media audit is a review process where you examine all your social media accounts to evaluate performance, consistency, and strategy.

questions.question: What does CTR stand for in digital marketing?
questions.explanation: CTR (Click Through Rate) is the percentage of people who click on your ad or link after seeing it.

questions.question: What is the best type of content for Instagram engagement?
options: Visual content — images and short videos
questions.explanation: Instagram is a visual platform. High-quality images, Reels, and short videos consistently get the highest engagement rates.
